items_for_public.reason: Official Kinitos-era public appearance while Steven Woods was CEO.
items_for_public.summary: University of Saskatchewan page records a 2003 P.G. Sorenson lecture by Steven Woods while he was CEO of Kinitos and notes he left AOL in May 2002 to co-found the company.

items_for_public.reason: Official recent university page that neatly summarizes Steven Woods' connection to Quack.com, Kinitos, and NeoEdge Networks.
items_for_public.summary: University of Waterloo's 2023 Research Discovery Days page describes Steven Woods as co-founder of Quack.com, Kinitos, and NeoEdge Networks and highlights the cross-border teams he built.

items_for_public.reason: Potentially useful closing milestone for a short Steven career-timeline summary of the NeoEdge era.
items_for_public.summary: Game Developer reported Double Fusion's December 15, 2011 acquisition of NeoEdge, marking a clear late-stage milestone for the company line.

open_questions.question: Confirm whether the top-level hub wants only official institutional pages for Steven-facing career summaries, or whether trade-press milestone items like the 2011 acquisition should also be surfaced.
Capture a local or archived copy of the CNET Yahoo Games article before promoting that 2008 cluster beyond deferred status.

Patent assignment records now confirm that at least some NeoEdge / Blue Noodle intellectual-property assets passed into Double Fusion in 2011, but longer-term public evidence of product or brand use inside Double Fusion is still thin.
No distinct pre-2006 Kinitos-only funding source has been verified yet; current investor history appears only in later NeoEdge/Blue Noodle financing summaries.
